title: Will it be confirmed that Graham Platner is the Democratic nominee for U.S. Senate in Maine on July 31, 2026?

current.lean_note: no +EV value bet at the ask (my prob 0%, modal NO) — staying out.
current.rationale_summary: The base rate for a candidate withdrawing from a Senate nomination within the last few weeks before official confirmation is low but non-zero, given historical cases of similar withdrawals in politics. The key evidence includes Platner's official withdrawal after facing allegations of sexual assault; these allegations are significant and have led to substantial support being withdrawn from him. Gi
key_drivers: A candidate facing sexual assault allegations
Troy Jackson moving toward nomination; the likelihood of finding another viable candidate so close to the deadline
reference_classes: Historical cases of Senate nominees replacing and withdrawing before confirmation deadlines

history.lean_note: no +EV value bet at the ask (my prob 0%, modal NO) — staying out.
history.rationale_summary: Multiple credible sources from July 14-17, 2026, confirm that Graham Platner has withdrawn from the Senate nomination, with Maine Democrats actively seeking a replacement. While the exact timeline of his withdrawal is unclear, the consensus across multiple reputable outlets indicates that he is no longer the nominee.
